title: CIA UFO Reading Room
summary: Declassified CIA documents on UFO investigations and sightings from multiple decades, providing historical context for government UFO research programs.
use: Establishes the historical precedent of government UFO documentation and investigation protocols that inform Terminal Varginha's fictional classified file structure.

title: FBI Records: The Vault - UFO
summary: FBI investigative files on UFO phenomena, including witness reports and inter-agency communications about unidentified aerial phenomena.
use: Provides authentic examples of government documentation style and classification levels used in Terminal Varginha's interface design.

title: Brazilian UFO Research - UFO.com.br
publisher: Brazilian Center for Flying Saucer Research
summary: Comprehensive Portuguese-language database of UFO sightings in Brazil, including detailed coverage of the Varginha incident with witness testimonies and media reports.
use: Primary source for Varginha incident details, witness accounts, and timeline events that form the narrative foundation of the game.

title: National UFO Reporting Center Database
summary: Civilian-operated database collecting UFO sighting reports from across the world, providing standardized report formats and witness testimony archives.
use: Reference for civilian UFO reporting structures and witness testimony formats used in Terminal Varginha's fictional document design.

title: NICAP UFO Evidence Archive
publisher: National Investigations Committee on Aerial Phenomena
summary: Historical archive of UFO investigations conducted by civilian researchers, including case files, witness reports, and analysis of military and government documents.
use: Informs the game's approach to presenting UFO evidence and the civilian investigation perspective alongside government files.

title: The Varginha UFO Incident
summary: Investigative journalism piece covering the Varginha incident with interviews, analysis of witness credibility, and examination of official Brazilian military response.
use: Provides journalistic framework for how Terminal Varginha presents competing narratives and evaluates witness testimony reliability.

title: CIA Reading Room: Brazil UFO Documents
summary: Search interface for declassified CIA documents related to UFO activity and intelligence operations in Brazil during the Cold War era. Includes reports on South American UFO sightings, inter-agency communications, and assessments of foreign government UFO investigation programs.
use: Establishes the historical context of U.S. intelligence interest in Brazilian UFO phenomena, informing the game's fictional inter-agency dynamics and classified reporting structures.

title: FBI UFO Files - Part 1 of 16
summary: First section of FBI's comprehensive UFO investigation archive containing case files from the 1940s-1970s, including field reports, witness interviews, scientific analysis requests, and inter-agency correspondence with Air Force UFO investigation programs like Project Blue Book.
use: Provides authentic examples of field investigation procedures, witness interview formats, and evidence documentation standards that inform Terminal Varginha's fictional case file structure and interrogation transcripts.

title: Brazilian Military UFO Protocol History
publisher: Brazilian Center for Flying Saucer Research
summary: Documentation of Brazilian Armed Forces official UFO investigation policies from 1950s-1990s, including the 1986 'Night of the UFOs' official response and the pattern of initial military engagement followed by categorical denial in high-profile cases.
use: Informs the game's depiction of Brazilian military response protocols, inter-service jurisdictional conflicts, and the strategic pattern of deny-then-declassify that characterizes official handling of extraordinary incidents.
